title: Software that does 'human' things
content: Artificial intelligence is software that performs tasks that normally require human-like judgment or pattern recognition: recognizing a face, translating a sentence, recommending a movie, or spotting fraud. AI is not one single technology. It is an umbrella term for many techniques that aim to perform these kinds of tasks.
next: If AI is the umbrella, what sits underneath it?

title: Russian dolls: AI, ML, DL
content: Think of nesting dolls. The largest doll is AI: the broad category. Inside it sits machine learning: systems that learn patterns from examples instead of relying only on hand-written rules. Inside that sits deep learning: a type of machine learning built with large layered neural networks. Every deep learning system is machine learning, and every machine learning system belongs under AI, but the reverse is not true.
next: What was AI like before machines could learn?

title: The old way: hand-written rules
content: Early AI was built from rules written by programmers. A spam filter might say: if the email contains the word 'lottery', block it. This works until spammers write 'l0ttery'. Rule-based systems are brittle - every exception needs a human to write another rule, and the real world has endless exceptions.
next: So how do you handle a problem with endless exceptions?

title: The new way: learning from examples
content: Machine learning flips the approach. Instead of writing every rule, you show the system many examples - for instance spam emails and normal emails - and it learns patterns from that data. It is like teaching recognition by repeated examples instead of giving a full written rulebook.
next: What happens when the patterns are too complex for simple learning?

title: Deep learning: layers upon layers
content: Deep learning uses neural networks - software loosely inspired by patterns of connected neurons - stacked in many layers. Early layers may detect simple features such as edges in an image, while deeper layers combine those features into more complex patterns. Deep learning works especially well when data is large and messy, such as images, speech, and language.
next: For decades, all of this AI had one thing in common. Then something changed.

title: Classic AI sorts, generative AI creates
content: Much older AI was built mainly for classification and prediction: Is this spam? What is in this photo? What price might this house sell for? Generative AI does something different: it produces new content such as text, images, code, or audio. That shift from mainly sorting and predicting to also generating is one reason recent AI feels different to users.
next: Why did this leap happen now and not twenty years ago?

title: Three ingredients came together
content: Generative AI needed several things to mature at the same time: enormous amounts of text and images, large amounts of computing power, and better model designs such as the transformer architecture introduced in 2017. When these ingredients aligned, model capability improved very quickly.
next: But is any of this actually 'intelligent'?

title: What 'intelligence' means here
content: AI systems do not understand or want things in the human sense. They are highly effective pattern-processing systems. A calculator can outperform a human at arithmetic without being human-like, and AI systems can outperform humans on some pattern tasks in a similar way. Keeping this distinction in mind helps reduce both hype and confusion.
next: How capable are these systems, really?

title: Narrow, not general
content: Today's AI is narrow: brilliant inside the task it was built for, helpless outside it. A chess engine cannot drive a car. A language model cannot smell smoke. The sci-fi idea of one system that matches humans at everything - artificial general intelligence - remains a research goal, not a product you can use.
next: You already use narrow AI more than you think.

title: AI is already everywhere
content: Your phone unlocks with your face, your inbox filters spam, your map reroutes around traffic, your streaming app guesses your taste. All of it is AI, running quietly in the background for years. The new wave - the chatbots and image generators - is simply the first AI you talk to directly.
next: And that new wave is built on one specific invention: the large language model.
